type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize() (lines ~1051-1116 in src/PIL/Image.py) to sort palette colors after quantization (by brightness/RGB). Will modify the quantize() method signature and add sorting logic before returning. Also adding tests to Tests/test_image_quantize.py.

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding error_threshold param to Image.quantize() (Image.py lines ~1051-1116). Will add param to signature, docstring, and body logic to iteratively reduce colors / stop early once MSE falls below threshold. What construct(s) are you touching?

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: quantize() method
code:     def quantize(
        self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1,
        error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None
    ):
        self.load()

        if method is None:
            # defaults:
            method = MEDIANCUT
            if self.mode == "RGBA":
                method = FASTOCTREE

        if self.mode == "RGBA" and method not in (FASTOCTREE, LIBIMAGEQUANT):
            # Caller specified an invalid mode.
            raise ValueError(
                "Fast Octree (method == 2) and libimagequant (method == 3) "
                "are the only valid methods for quantizing RGBA images"
            )

        if palette:
            # use palette from reference image
            palette.load()
            if palette.mode != "P":
                raise ValueError("bad mode for palette image")
            if self.mode != "RGB" and self.mode != "L":
                raise ValueError(
                    "only RGB or L mode images can be quantized to a palette"
                )
            im = self.im.convert("P", dither, palette.im)
            new_im = self._new(im)
            new_im.palette = palette.palette.copy()
            return new_im

        im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))

        from . import ImagePalette

        mode = im.im.getpalettemode()
        im.palette = ImagePalette.ImagePalette(mode, im.im.getpalette(mode, mode))

        if sort_palette:
            im._sort_palette(sort_palette)

        return im
note: Both touch signature/docstring/body. Proposing param order: colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None (alphabetical for new kwargs). My part: sort_palette applied at the very end via a new _sort_palette() helper, only on the newly-computed-palette path (not the palette= reference path). Please send your exact code for error_threshold handling (signature placement + body) so we can merge into one method body.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: quantize() method
note: Here is my proposed error_threshold logic; please graft your sort_palette logic into this same method body (after palette assembly, before return) and send back the FULL merged method text so we converge. My planned signature: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=0, sort_palette=False): -- placing sort_palette param last so both new params coexist. error_threshold: binary-search on number of colors (1..colors) finding minimal count whose MSE (via ImageChops.difference histogram) <= error_threshold; falls back to full 'colors' if none meet threshold. Sending full draft code next in a follow-up DRAFT once you confirm param ordering, or reply with your sort_palette snippet now.

note: Confirmed order: colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, error_threshold=0, sort_palette=None (last). One correction: sort_palette must accept a STRING selecting the sort mode (e.g. "luminance" or "rgb"), not a bool, since we support multiple predefined sort methods. Falsy value (None/""​) = no sorting, for backward compatibility. My snippet to graft in the shared body, placed right after palette assembly and before return im (applies only on the newly-computed-palette path, not the palette= reference-image path):

code:     def quantize(
        self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1,
        error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None
    ):
        self.load()

        if method is None:
            # defaults:
            method = MEDIANCUT
            if self.mode == "RGBA":
                method = FASTOCTREE

        if self.mode == "RGBA" and method not in (FASTOCTREE, LIBIMAGEQUANT):
            # Caller specified an invalid mode.
            raise ValueError(
                "Fast Octree (method == 2) and libimagequant (method == 3) "
                "are the only valid methods for quantizing RGBA images"
            )

        if palette:
            # use palette from reference image
            palette.load()
            if palette.mode != "P":
                raise ValueError("bad mode for palette image")
            if self.mode != "RGB" and self.mode != "L":
                raise ValueError(
                    "only RGB or L mode images can be quantized to a palette"
                )
            im = self.im.convert("P", dither, palette.im)
            new_im = self._new(im)
            new_im.palette = palette.palette.copy()
            return new_im

        if error_threshold:
            im = self._quantize_to_error_threshold(colors, method, kmeans, error_threshold)
        else:
            im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))

        from . import ImagePalette

        mode = im.im.getpalettemode()
        im.palette = ImagePalette.ImagePalette(mode, im.im.getpalette(mode, mode))

        if sort_palette:
            im._sort_palette(sort_palette)

        return im
note: Merged: kept your exact param order/defaults (error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None) and your sort_palette call site. Inserted an if/else for error_threshold that calls a new helper _quantize_to_error_threshold(colors, method, kmeans, error_threshold) I'll define separately (non-overlapping, near quantize()). Please confirm this is byte-identical to what you'll write, then AGREE.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: quantize() docstring
code:         """
        Convert the image to 'P' mode with the specified number
        of colors.

        :param colors: The desired number of colors, <= 256
        :param method: :data:`MEDIANCUT` (median cut),
                       :data:`MAXCOVERAGE` (maximum coverage),
                       :data:`FASTOCTREE` (fast octree),
                       :data:`LIBIMAGEQUANT` (libimagequant; check support using
                       :py:func:`PIL.features.check_feature`
                       with ``feature="libimagequant"``).

                       By default, :data:`MEDIANCUT` will be used.

                       The exception to this is RGBA images. :data:`MEDIANCUT` and
                       :data:`MAXCOVERAGE` do not support RGBA images, so
                       :data:`FASTOCTREE` is used by default instead.
        :param kmeans: Integer
        :param palette: Quantize to the palette of given
                        :py:class:`PIL.Image.Image`.
        :param dither: Dithering method, used when converting from
           mode "RGB" to "P" or from "RGB" or "L" to "1".
           Available methods are :data:`NONE` or :data:`FLOYDSTEINBERG` (default).
           Default: 1 (legacy setting)
        :param error_threshold: The maximum acceptable mean square error (MSE)
           between the original image and the quantized result. If set, the
           number of colors used may be reduced below ``colors`` once the
           error falls below this threshold. If the threshold cannot be met
           within the ``colors`` limit, ``colors`` colors will be used.
           Default: 0 (disabled, use ``colors`` colors directly).
        :param sort_palette: The method used to sort the palette after
           quantization, e.g. ``"luminance"`` or ``"rgb"``. A falsy value
           (the default, ``None``) leaves the palette unsorted.
        :returns: A new image

        """
note: Noticed the agreed method body has NO docstring (goes straight from the ':' to self.load()), but the original quantize() has one. If we each add our own docstring text independently in that same slot, our patches will conflict. Proposing this merged docstring (adds :param error_threshold: and :param sort_palette: entries, keeping all original text unchanged) to sit between the signature and self.load(). Please review your :param sort_palette: wording and send AGREE or adjust.

All existing quantize tests still pass. Note: _quantize_to_error_threshold() is not yet defined in my copy of Image.py -- make sure you add it near quantize() so error_threshold branch works; my sort_palette path doesn't touch it (error_threshold defaults to 0/falsy so it's skipped in my tests).

files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implemented error_threshold: wrote the agreed quantize() body + docstring verbatim, added non-overlapping _quantize_to_error_threshold() helper (linear scan from 1..colors, returns first candidate whose MSE vs original <= error_threshold, falls back to full 'colors' if none qualify) placed right after quantize()/before copy(). Added 3 tests. All 10 existing + new tests pass locally. My patch references im._sort_palette(sort_palette) per our agreed body but doesn't define it (guarded by 'if sort_palette' which defaults falsy, so my patch alone is self-consistent).
